---
title: "Dask DataFrame: A Practical Guide"
---

Welcome to this blog on **Dask DataFrame**, a Python library for scalable, parallel data processing.

Dask extends the familiar Pandas API to handle datasets that are too large to fit in memory, using lazy evaluation and parallel execution across multiple cores or distributed clusters.

### What You Will Find Here

- An overview of Dask DataFrame and its key features
- Hands-on code examples using a million-row sales dataset
- Practical use cases for data analysis, preprocessing, and real-time processing

Head over to the [Blog](blog.qmd) to read the full post.

### About

This blog was built as part of an academic assignment on scalable data processing with Python. It uses [Quarto](https://quarto.org/) for rendering and is hosted on GitHub Pages.
